mission.title: flux: flux bootstrap github - Scopes
mission.description: flux bootstrap github - Scopes. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: flux bootstrap github - Scopes

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
The documentation doesn't mention which scopes are needed for the Github PAT.  Is it possible to add this information?

steps.title: Apply the fix for flux bootstrap github - Scopes
steps.description:
Resolves https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/4412 and https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/2329.
```yaml
Read access to metadata
 Read and Write access to administration and code
```

steps.title: Confirm flux bootstrap github - Scopes is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: Resolves https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/4412 and https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/2329.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
Read access to metadata
 Read and Write access to administration and code

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
